response.justification: Bogdan Szabo demonstrates strong full-stack and open-source development experience, with exposure to geospatial data and distributed systems. However, his recent work lacks direct alignment with the core requirements of modern C++/Rust for routing tiling pipelines, and his geospatial experience is more web/mobile-focused than low-latency data processing.
strengths: Extensive experience with distributed systems and high-performance applications (e.g., DLang at Sociomantic, real-time metadata handling).
Open-source contributions to geospatial platforms (e.g., GISCollective) show familiarity with mapping data and collaboration tools.
Strong problem-solving and experimentation mindset, evidenced by workshops on testing and functional programming.
concerns: Primary expertise in TypeScript/JavaScript and web/mobile stacks, with limited recent C++/Rust experience (last noted in 2015 at National Instruments).
Geospatial work (e.g., Green Map) leans toward web GIS rather than low-latency routing data pipelines or navigation systems.
No explicit experience with routing tiles, traffic signal integration, or production-grade geospatial data optimization.
